title: Oxymeris cinnamomea
intro: Oxymeris cinnamomea, also known as Cinnamon tree or Wild Cinnamon, is a small, tropical tree native to the tropical forests of the Indo-Pacific region. The bark and leaves of Oxymeris cinnamomea have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide health benefits such as pain relief and improved wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oxymeris cinnamomea is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used to make cinnamon oil, which is used topically for skin conditions and as a local anesthetic.
